Example Blog About

Personal style guide

Style emerges from consistency, and having a style opens your imagination. Your mind should be flexible, but your process should be repeatable.

A style can be a system, a pattern, a set of personal guidelines. Here are a few of mine:

  • I wear monochromatic clothing without logos
  • I use a specific date format everywhere
  • I pluralize tag and folder names (e.g. groups not group)
  • I use plain text files for all my writing
  • I ask myself reflection questions every year
  • I meal prep lunches every week, and exercise twice a week
  • I write short essays, less than 500 words

Collect constraints you enjoy. Unusual constraints make things more fun. You can always change them later.

Having a style collapses hundreds of future decisions into one, and gives you focus.
